title: CDRS Trace v0.2
description: Full execution history of a dispute resolution scenario (CDRS v0.2). Backward-compatible with v0.1 traces; expected_semantics is optional.

scenario_id.description: Scenario identifier (matches the scenario input file id)

trace_kind.description: Content tags used to select semantic check sets in TraceEquivalence.t.sol

expected_semantics.description: Semantic expectations for the trace. All sub-blocks are optional; omitting a sub-block suppresses that class of check.

resolutions.description: CDRS v0.1 resolution summary keyed by wf alias (retained for backward compat)

resolution_expected.description: Expected resolution semantics. Required when trace_kind contains 'dispute-resolution'.

beneficiary.description: Who received the funds: 'seller' | 'buyer' | 'settled'. Absent when unresolved.

resolver.description: Agent id of the party who executed resolution. Absent when unresolved.

pending_settlement_created.description: Whether a pending-settlement object was created (exists in world or was later executed)

escalation_expected.description: Expected escalation semantics. Present only when escalate_dispute appears in the trace.

level.description: Dispute escalation level at trace end

max_level_reached.description: Maximum escalation level observed across the trace

tier.description: Resolver tier corresponding to the final level

attempted.description: Whether escalation was attempted (always true when this block is present)

accepted.description: Whether at least one escalation action succeeded (:ok)

rejected.description: Whether at least one escalation action was rejected

participation_expected.description: Expected participation semantics. Present for any trace with a dispute raised.

resolution_actor.description: Agent id who last called execute_resolution successfully

settlement_actor.description: Agent id who called execute_pending_settlement. Absent if none.

authorized_participant.description: Whether the resolution actor's call succeeded (:ok)

timing_expected.description: Expected timing semantics. Present for any trace with a dispute raised.

auto_cancel_triggered.description: Whether auto_cancel_disputed succeeded in the trace

within_resolution_window.description: Whether (resolution_time - dispute_open_time) <= max-dispute-duration. Present only when max-dispute-duration > 0 and resolution timestamp exists.

within_settlement_window.description: Whether (settlement_time - resolution_time) <= appeal-window-duration. Present only when appeal-window-duration > 0 and settlement timestamp exists.

pending_delay_seconds.description: settlement_time - resolution_time in seconds. Present only when both timestamps exist.
